title: Project Evidence Maintenance Prioritization
description: Prioritize which evidence-maintenance and planning fronts deserve immediate attention once the sidecar project lanes are accepted, while keeping frozen and speculative fronts explicit.
question: Given the current accepted sidecar and documentation truths, which evidence-maintenance and planning fronts should Golem prioritize now, which should wait, and which should remain frozen or untouched?
expected_signals: readiness real de worker externo no alcanza hoy

sources.label: oc_cli
sources.notes: Public baseline for command-driven operational work.

sources.label: oc_browser
sources.notes: Public browser surface that remains informative but not decisive for this host.

local_sources.label: local_current_state
local_sources.notes: Current state summary and accepted lanes.

decision_criteria.label: Versioned Truth Density
decision_criteria.description: The front should have strong versioned evidence already present in docs, runbooks or handoffs.
decision_criteria.notes: Dense versioned evidence is a strong prioritization signal.

decision_criteria.label: Reentry Speed
decision_criteria.description: The front should make future retomes faster and less ambiguous.
decision_criteria.notes: Favor fronts that help the project resume cleanly.

decision_criteria.label: Project Reuse
decision_criteria.description: The front should stay reusable across future project questions rather than only one demo.

decision_criteria.label: Expansion Safety
decision_criteria.description: The front should not quietly reopen blocked or oversold surfaces.
evidence_terms: readiness real de worker externo no alcanza hoy

priority_buckets.label: NOW
priority_buckets.description: Work immediately in the next tranche.

priority_buckets.label: NEXT
priority_buckets.description: Valuable follow-on after the NOW tranche.

priority_buckets.label: LATER
priority_buckets.description: Keep visible but do not advance now.

priority_buckets.label: FROZEN
priority_buckets.description: Known blocked debt or an explicitly frozen front.

priority_buckets.label: REOPEN_ONLY_IF
priority_buckets.description: Only revisit under explicit fresh evidence.

project_fronts.label: Truth Docs and Handoff
project_fronts.description: Maintain the versioned truth package that makes the project easy to re-enter without re-litigating old debates.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: The repo already uses CURRENT_STATE, capability matrix, runbook and handoff as the truth surface.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Keep the truth surface sharp and reusable for future tranches.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: Low relative cost because the repo already treats these documents as canonical evidence.
risk_hints: Can slip into paperwork if it stops serving real project questions.
Can become repetitive if not anchored to verified changes.
preconditions: Only update after verified truths or new lanes actually land.
Keep the docs compact and aligned with the scripts.
kill_criteria: Pause if the docs stop reflecting real verified changes.
Pause if documentation work starts replacing tranche execution.
project_fronts.recommended_action: Keep truth docs and handoff in NOW so every later tranche starts from the same accepted reality.
project_fronts.notes: This is the most durable maintenance front.

project_fronts.label: Prioritization and Recommendation Layers
project_fronts.description: Keep evolving the upper planning lanes that turn evidence into bounded project moves.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: These layers already exist and are ready to answer bounded project questions.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Use the lane stack to keep future project decisions auditable and scoped.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: Medium relative cost because the lanes are real and reusable, but they should not expand without producing concrete project outputs.
risk_hints: Can become meta-work if not grounded on concrete project questions.
Can overgrow into a framework if every tranche adds another abstraction layer.
preconditions: Each new layer must answer a concrete project question.
kill_criteria: Pause if the lane stack grows faster than the project decisions it supports.
Pause if the work starts to duplicate dossier, decision or recommendation behavior.
project_fronts.recommended_action: Keep these layers in NEXT and only extend them when the project has a concrete prioritization or recommendation question to answer.
project_fronts.notes: High leverage, but should remain bounded.

project_fronts.label: Gateway and Channels Reference
project_fronts.description: Broaden the public planning baseline toward gateway and channels after the evidence package is stable.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: Useful public surface, but not the most urgent evidence-maintenance front.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Keep a documented follow-on baseline for broader OpenClaw planning.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: Medium relative cost because the public surface is broad and useful, but not urgent for maintaining the accepted truths.
risk_hints: Can dilute the evidence package into a general product roadmap.
Can distract from the lane stack that is already producing concrete answers.
preconditions: Keep this front scoped as a public reference follow-on.
kill_criteria: Pause if this front becomes a general expansion of scope.
Pause if it tries to replace the accepted sidecar project lane.
project_fronts.recommended_action: Keep gateway/channels reference work in LATER until the evidence-maintenance tranche is settled.

project_fronts.label: Browser Native Recovery
project_fronts.description: Any attempt to recover the native OpenClaw browser lane.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: Explicitly blocked debt and not the current project lane.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Keep browser-native recovery frozen until new evidence exists.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: High relative cost because the current accepted truth still says the native browser lane is blocked debt.
risk_hints: Can absorb effort without producing a trustworthy operator lane.
Can destabilize the truth package by re-litigating settled debt.
preconditions: Require new verified PASS evidence before reopening.
Do not confuse public browser docs with host-local proof.
kill_criteria: Stop immediately if there is no fresh PASS evidence.
Stop if the work starts depending on speculation about raw listeners or ambient Chrome state.
project_fronts.recommended_action: Keep browser-native recovery frozen and outside the evidence-maintenance tranche.

project_fronts.label: Worker Stack Reopen
project_fronts.description: Reopening external worker, orchestration and bridge surfaces.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: Documented but not ready to sell as stable.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Only reopen if the worker readiness evidence changes materially.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: High relative cost because the current versioned truth still says worker readiness is not there.
risk_hints: Can pull multiple unstable dependencies back into scope at once.
Can turn a bounded evidence tranche into a wide runtime chase.
preconditions: Require fresh PASS evidence from the worker orchestration verify stack.
Keep this front subordinated to proven browser and evidence lanes.
kill_criteria: Stop if worker work is being justified by governance prose alone.
Stop if the front is reopened before relay/task API/bridge checks turn green.

REOPEN_ONLY_IF: readiness real de worker externo no alcanza hoy

project_fronts.recommended_action: Only reopen worker stack work if the self-check and verify evidence materially improve.

project_fronts.label: Host Control Storytelling
project_fronts.description: Any attempt to frame the project as having total host or desktop control today.
project_fronts.current_state_hint: Explicitly rejected by the versioned truth package.
project_fronts.intended_outcome: Keep host-control storytelling out of the evidence-maintenance lane.
project_fronts.relative_cost_note: High relative cost because this front creates trust risk with little evidentiary support.
risk_hints: High chance of overselling what the repo can actually prove.
Can contaminate otherwise honest prioritization outputs.
preconditions: None under the current accepted truths.
A future reopening would require a separate host-control proof program.
kill_criteria: Stop if the language starts implying full host control.
Stop if host-control claims are used to justify unrelated work.

DO_NOT_TOUCH: Read-side del desktop no significa control host total.
Vender escritorio completo o control host total.

project_fronts.recommended_action: Do not touch host-control storytelling in this tranche.
